userrights-lookup-user: Used as a legend for a fieldset when managing user rights ([[Special:UserRights]])
editusergroup: Button name, in page [[Special:Userrights]], in the section named {{MediaWiki:userrights-lookup-user}}. The username or gender of the user is not known when this message is displayed.
editinguser:
Appears on [[Special:UserRights]]. Parameters:
* $1 - a plaintext username
* $2 - user tool links. e.g. "(Talk | contribs | block | send email)"

Related messages:
* {{msg-mw|viewinguserrights}}
viewinguserrights:
Appears on [[Special:UserRights]]. Parameters:
* $1 - a plaintext username
* $2 - user tool links. e.g. "(Talk | contribs | block | send email)"

Related messages:
* {{msg-mw|editinguser}}
userrights-editusergroup:
Used as a legend for a fieldset in the form at [[Special:UserRights]].

Parameter:
* $1 - A username, can be used for GENDER

Related messages:
* {{msg-mw|userrights-viewusergroup}}
userrights-viewusergroup:
Used as a legend for a fieldset in the form at [[Special:UserRights]].

Parameter:
* $1 - (Optional) a username, can be used for GENDER

Related messages:
* {{msg-mw|userrights-editusergroup}}
saveusergroups:
Button text when editing user groups.
Parameters:
* $1 - username, for GENDER support
userrights-groupsmember:
Used when editing user groups in [[Special:Userrights]].

The message is followed by a list of group names.

Parameters:
* $1 - (Optional) the number of items in the list following the message, for PLURAL
* $2 - (Optional) the username, for GENDER
userrights-groupsmember-auto:
Used when editing user groups in [[Special:Userrights]]. The message is followed by a list of group names.

"Implicit" is for groups that the user was automatically added to (such as "autoconfirmed"); cf. {{msg-mw|userrights-groupsmember}}

Parameters:
* $1 - (Optional) the number of items in the list following the message, for PLURAL
* $2 - (Optional) the username, for GENDER
userrights-systemuser:
Indicates that a user account is a system user. Parameters:
* $1 - (Optional) a username, can be used for GENDER
userrights-groups-help: Instructions displayed on [[Special:UserRights]]. Parameters: * $1 - A username, can be used for GENDER Note for section marked #: * "Put back" is a phrasal verb meaning "to change the date of an event so that it happens later than planned". * "Bring forward" is a phrasal verb meaning "to change the date of an event so that it happens earlier than planned".
userrights-reason:
Text beside log field when editing user groups

{{Identical|Reason}}
userrights-watchuser: This is an option on [[Special:UserRights]] to watch the user page and talk page of the changed user.
userrights-no-interwiki: Error message when editing user groups
userrights-nodatabase:
Error message when editing user groups.

"Local" means databases/wikis of the same farm/cluster; that is, meta, enwiki, dewiki, commons, etc are all local databases of the Wikimedia Foundation.

See [{{canonicalurl:meta:Special:Log|type=rights}} meta:Special:Log?type=rights] for a usage of local databases: username@barwiki

Parameters:
* $1 - database name
userrights-restricted-group-warning: Used on [[Special:Userrights]] and related pages to indicate that a user group has restrictions on who can be added to the group. This message is shown by default if there is not a more specific message for a particular group.
userrights-restricted-group-warning-priv: Used on [[Special:Userrights]] and related pages to indicate that a user group has restrictions that will be evaluated on form submission, so that adding user to group may fail even if the checkbox is enabled. This message is shown by default if there is not a more specific message for a particular group.
userrights-changeable-col:
Used when editing user groups in [[Special:Userrights]].

The message is the head of a column of group assignments.

Parameters:
* $1 - (Optional) for PLURAL use, the number of items in the column following the message. Avoid PLURAL, if your language allows that.
userrights-unchangeable-col:
Used when editing user groups in [[Special:Userrights]].

The message is the head of a column of group assignments.

Parameters:
* $1 - (Optional) for PLURAL use, the number of items in the column following the message. Avoid PLURAL, if your language allows that.
userrights-expiry-current:
Indicates when a user's membership of a user group expires.

Parameters:
* $1 - time and date of expiry
* $2 - date of expiry
* $3 - time of expiry
{{Identical|Expire}}
userrights-expiry-none: Indicates that a user's membership of a user group lasts indefinitely, and does not expire.
userrights-expiry-existing:
Shows the existing expiry time in the drop-down menu underneath the individual user right on Special:UserRights.

Parameters:
* $1 - Date and time of the existing expiry (unused)
* $2 - date of the existing expiry
* $3 - time of the existing expiry

See also:
* {{msg-mw|protect-existing-expiry}}
userrights-expiry-options:
Options for the duration of the user group membership.

{{doc-mediawiki-options-list}}
userrights-invalid-expiry:
Error message on [[Special:UserRights]].

Parameters:
* $1 - group name
userrights-expiry-in-past:
Error message on [[Special:UserRights]] when the user types an expiry date that has already passed.

Parameters:
* $1 - group name
userrights-no-group: Used as error message on [[Special:UserRights]] when the user is temporary user.
userrights-conflict: Shown on [[Special:UserRights]] if the target's rights have been changed since the form was loaded.
userrights-unable-to-change:
Error message on [[Special:UserRights]] when performer attempted to change some groups they are unable to change.

Parameters:
* $1 - List of groups that cannot be changed, together with a reason
* $2 - Name of the target user (for GENDER)
* $3 - number of list entries in parameter $1
userrights-unable-to-change-row:
{{optional}}
A message used for making list items in {{msg-mw|userrights-unable-to-change}}.

Parameters:
* $1 - Name of a user group
* $2 - Reason why user cannot be added to or removed from the group
userrights-insufficient-rights: One of the possible reasons in {{msg-mw|userrights-unable-to-change}}, stating that the performer does not have sufficient rights to change target's membership in a given group
group-temp:
{{doc-group|temp}}
The name of the group that contains auto-created temporary users.
group-autoconfirmed:
{{doc-group|autoconfirmed}}
On Wikimedia sites, autoconfirmed users are users which are older than 4 days. After those 4 days, they have more rights.
group-all:
The name of the user group that contains all users, including anonymous users

{{Identical|All}}
right-read:
{{doc-right|read}}
Basic right to read any page.
right-createpage:
{{doc-right|createpage}}
Basic right to create pages. The right to edit discussion/talk pages is {{msg-mw|right-createtalk}}.
right-createtalk:
{{doc-right|createtalk}}
Basic right to create discussion/talk pages. The right to edit other pages is {{msg-mw|right-createpage}}.
right-createaccount:
{{doc-right|createaccount}}
The right to [[Special:CreateAccount|create a user account]].
right-autocreateaccount:
{{doc-right|autocreateaccount}}
The right to automatically create an account from an external source (e.g. CentralAuth).
right-minoredit:
{{doc-right|minoredit}}
The right to use the "This is a minor edit" checkbox. See {{msg-mw|minoredit}} for the message used for that checkbox.
right-purge: {{doc-right|purge}} The right to use &action=purge in the URL.
right-nominornewtalk:
{{doc-right|nominornewtalk}}
If someone with this right (bots by default) edits a user talk page and marks it as minor (requires {{msg-mw|right-minoredit}}), the user will not get a notification "You have new messages".
right-ipblock-exempt:
{{doc-right|ipblock-exempt}}
This user automatically bypasses IP blocks, auto-blocks and range blocks - so I presume - but I am uncertain
right-markbotedits: {{doc-right|markbotedits}} A user with this right can mark a roll-back edit as a bot edit by adding &bot=1 to the URL (not by default).
right-noratelimit:
{{doc-right|noratelimit}}
The rate limits have no effect on the groups that have this right. Rate limits is a restriction that you can only do X actions (edits, moves, etc.) in Y number of seconds (set by [[mw:Manual:$wgRateLimits|$wgRateLimits]]).
right-pagelang:
{{Doc-right|pagelang}}
Right to change page language on Special:PageLanguage
right-ignore-restricted-groups:
{{doc-right|ignore-restricted-groups}}

Restricted groups are groups that expect some conditions about the user or performer to be met, for example the number of edits, or the age of the account. This right allows the performer to ignore those restrictions when changing user rights, provided that the group configuration allows for that.
grantriskgroup-vandalism: Invisible description of the "vandalism" grant risk group icon at [[Special:BotPasswords]], for screen readers.
grantriskgroup-security: Invisible description of the "security" grant risk group icon at [[Special:BotPasswords]], for screen readers.
grantriskgroup-internal: Invisible description of the "internal" grant risk group icon at [[Special:BotPasswords]], for screen readers.
action-userrights:
{{Doc-action|userrights}}

This action allows editing of all of the "user rights", not just the rights of the group "all users".
action-ignore-restricted-groups:
{{Doc-action|ignore-restricted-groups}}

Restricted groups are groups that expect some conditions about the user or performer to be met, for example the number of edits, or the age of the account. This right allows the performer to ignore those restrictions when changing user rights, provided that the group configuration allows for that.
listgrouprights: The name of the special page [[Special:ListGroupRights]].
listgrouprights-summary:
The description used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]].

Refers to {{msg-mw|Listgrouprights-helppage}}.
listgrouprights-key: Appears above the table on the [[Special:ListGroupRights]] page. "Granted" and "Revoked" should be translated in the present tense: "is granted" and not "was granted".
listgrouprights-rights:
The title of the column in the table, about user rights (like you can ''edit'' this page).
{{Identical|Right}}
listgrouprights-helppage: The link used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]]. Just translate "Group rights", and '''leave the "Help:" namespace exactly as it is'''.
listgrouprights-members: Used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] and [[Special:Statistics]] as a link to [[Special:ListUsers|Special:ListUsers/"group"]], a list of members in that group.
listgrouprights-restricted:
Text displayed on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] when a group is restricted.

Parameters:
* $1 - anchor where the restrictions for this group can be seen
listgrouprights-removegroup:
This is an individual right for groups, used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]].
* $1 - an enumeration of group names
* $2 - the number of group names in $1
See also:
* {{msg-mw|listgrouprights-addgroup}}
listgrouprights-addgroup-self:
This is an individual right for groups, used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]].
* $1 - the group names
* $2 - the number of group names in $1
{{Related|Listgrouprights}}
listgrouprights-removegroup-self:
This is an individual right for groups, used on [[Special:ListGroupRights]].
* $1 - the group names
* $2 - the number of group names in $1
{{Related|Listgrouprights}}
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-header: Shown on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] as the header for the group restrictions table.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-config: Shown on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] as the column header for the group restrictions table.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-memberc: Displayed inside individual cell of the group restrictions table. It's followed by a list of conditions that the group member must meet.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-updater: Displayed inside individual cell of the group restrictions table. It's followed by a list of conditions that the user adding someone to this group must meet.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-bypassa:
A notice informing that users with a specific right can bypass the group restrictions displayed above in the same table cell.

Parameters:
* $1 - the description of the right, {{msg-mw|right-ignore-restricted-groups}}
* $2 - the internal name of the right, "ignore-restricted-groups", wrapped in a <nowiki><code></nowiki> tag
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-autodem: A notice informing that group members will be automatically removed from the group if they no longer meet the group restrictions displayed above in the same table cell.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-op-and:
Displayed as a list item for the group restrictions on [[Special:ListGroupRights]]. It represents logical AND operator.

Parameters:
* $1 - a list of conditions, rendered as an unordered list.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-op-or:
Displayed as a list item for the group restrictions on [[Special:ListGroupRights]]. It represents logical OR operator.

Parameters:
* $1 - a list of conditions, rendered as an unordered list.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-op-xor:
Displayed as a list item for the group restrictions on [[Special:ListGroupRights]]. It represents logical XOR operator.

Parameters:
* $1 - a list of conditions, rendered as an unordered list.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-op-nand:
Displayed as a list item for the group restrictions on [[Special:ListGroupRights]]. It represents logical NAND operator.

Parameters:
* $1 - a list of conditions, rendered as an unordered list.
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-ed:
A text representation of APCOND_EDITCOUNT condition, which tests if the user made at least the specified number of edits.

Parameters:
* $1 - the minimal number of edits
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-ag:
A text representation of APCOND_AGE condition, which tests if the user's account was created at least the specified time ago.

Parameters:
* $1 - the minimal age of the account
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-em: A text representation of APCOND_EMAILCONFIRMED condition, which tests if the user has confirmed email address
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-in:
A text representation of APCOND_INGROUPS condition, which tests if the user belongs to all the specified groups.

Parameters:
* $1 - the number of groups that are tested
* $2 - the list of groups (rendered inline)
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-is:
A text representation of APCOND_ISIP condition, which tests if the user connects from the speficied IP address.

Parameters:
* $1 - the expected IP address
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-ip:
A text representation of APCOND_IPINRANGE condition, which tests if the user connects from an IP address that belongs to the specified range.

Parameters:
* $1 - the expected IP range
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-ag:
A text representation of APCOND_AGE_FROM_EDIT condition, which tests if the user made their first edit at least the specified time ago.

Parameters:
* $1 - the minimal time that should have passed since the user's first edit
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-bl: A text representation of APCOND_BLOCKED condition, which tests if the user is currently sitewide blocked
listgrouprights-restrictedgroups-cond-is: A text representation of APCOND_ISBOT condition, which tests if the user has the bot right
listgrouprights-namespaceprotection-head: Shown on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] as the header for the namespace restrictions table.
listgrouprights-namespaceprotection-name:
Shown on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] as the 'namespace' column header for the namespace restrictions table.
{{Identical|Namespace}}
listgrouprights-namespaceprotection-rest: Shown on [[Special:ListGroupRights]] as the "right(s) allowing user to edit" column header for the namespace restrictions table.
restrictedgroups-autodemotion-log-reason:
Reason used in a log entry for automatic removal of groups that the user is no longer eligible for.

Parameters:
* $1 - user name (for GENDER)
* $2 - number of user's groups that are being removed
listgrants:
The name of the special page [[Special:ListGrants]].
{{Identical|Grant}}
listgrants-summary:
Explanatory text shown at the top of the grant/rights mapping table.

Refers to {{msg-mw|Listgrouprights-helppage}}.
listgrants-grant:
Used as table header for the grant/rights mapping table.
{{Identical|Grant}}
listgrants-rights:
Used as table header for the grant/rights mapping table.
{{Identical|Right}}
